toc_title: Using TLS with a managed cache
title: What are the configuration settings for TLS? - Azure Managed Redis
display_title: Configure TLS settings for Azure Managed Redis connections
description: Learn about the TLS protocol configuration and how to use TLS to securely communicate with Azure Managed Redis instances.
summary: Transport Layer Security (TLS) is a cryptographic protocol that provides secure communication over a network. Azure Managed Redis supports TLS on all tiers. When you create a service that uses an Azure Managed Redis instance, we strongly encourage you to connect using TLS. Important Azure Managed Redis only supports TLS 1.2 and 1.3. TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are not supported.
classification_reason: TLS configuration content includes supported protocol versions (1.2, 1.3 only), cipher and protocol settings, and possibly client configuration examples. These are concrete security configuration details specific to Azure Managed Redis.
